Know About Me

Design thinker.
Cultural observer.

Hi, I'm Zainab! I'm a BBA student exploring the intersection of design, culture, and consumer behaviour. My work focuses on how visual trends reflect deeper social patterns — identity, digital culture, and emotional consumption.

I'm particularly interested in editorial design, fashion communication, and cultural storytelling. I believe the most powerful designs don't just look good — they say something true about the world we live in.

Alongside my studies, I run ZucchiniBeads, my beaded jewellery brand — where business strategy meets handcraft and aesthetic identity.

✦ open to internships in fashion & media!

Sample Works

The projects
I made.

01 · Trend Report
2026: The New 2016?
Pages 1 & 2
A study of trend recurrence and cultural looping — exploring how fashion cycles back to the mid-2010s. The rise of maximalism, trinketcore, layering, and over-accessorizing. We are not moving forward. We are looping.
Trend Analysis Maximalism Editorial Layout Consumer Behaviour
Click to view full ↗
01 · Trend Report (cont.)
Consumption as Identity & Digital Culture Loop
Pages 3 & 4
The modern consumer is no longer driven by necessity, but by narrative. Every purchase is a statement of personality, taste, and belonging. The internet has eliminated the concept of true novelty — algorithms recycle culture faster than we can define it.
Digital Culture Identity Consumer Psychology Nostalgia
Click to view full ↗
02 · Cultural Commentary
The World Is Burning, But The Party Continues
A visual commentary on the contrast between global crisis and digital escapism — the suffering is visible, and yet it fades beneath the noise.
Cultural Commentary Collage Design Visual Contrast
Click to view full ↗
03 · Editorial
The Strategy of Cute
How cuteness functions as identity, comfort, and visual strategy. Softness is no longer passive — it performs. Cuteness is not an escape from reality. It is a way of surviving it.
Editorial Design Identity & Culture Consumer Psychology
Click to view full ↗
04 · Fashion Editorial
Shushu/Tong SS26: A Study in Contemporary Femininity
A visual editorial exploring the balance between softness and structure. SS26 explores how beauty becomes an inner force that confronts chaos and uncertainty. Softness is intentional, and styling becomes a form of control.
Fashion Editorial Styling Analysis Composition Brand Study
Click to view full ↗
